description: A beginner course for developers and operations engineers encountering DevOps for the first time. Starting from the friction between development and operations teams, it covers DevOps culture and principles (CALMS), Git-based collaboration, CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and monitoring/observability — combining core concepts with foundational hands-on practice at every step.
summary: Beginning with what DevOps is and the culture behind it, you'll progress through Git workflows, CI (build and test automation), CD (deployment automation), IaC (Infrastructure as Code), and monitoring/observability — all paced so that complete beginners can follow along from start to finish.
target_audience: Complete beginners to DevOps — developers or operations engineers who have heard terms like containers and CI/CD but have never studied them systematically.

lectures.title: What Is DevOps — The Wall Between Dev and Ops, and Why It Matters
objectives: Understand the problems that arise when development and operations are siloed — deployment delays, blame-shifting, and organizational silos
Explain the core values DevOps aims to deliver: fast delivery, stability, and collaboration
Recognize that DevOps is a culture and a way of working, not merely a set of tools

lectures.title: DevOps Culture and Principles — CALMS, Collaboration, Automation, and Measurement
objectives: Understand the CALMS framework: Culture, Automation, Lean, Measurement, and Sharing
Explain the core DevOps principles of collaboration, automation, and continuous improvement
Understand the organizational and cultural changes that come with adopting DevOps through everyday examples

lectures.title: Version Control and Collaboration — Git Workflow Fundamentals
objectives: Understand why version control is the starting point for both collaboration and automation
Learn the basic Git collaboration flow: branching, committing, merging, and pull requests
Practice creating a branch and merging changes using basic Git commands

lectures.title: CI Fundamentals — Build and Test Automation with Continuous Integration
objectives: Understand the concept of Continuous Integration (CI) and the rationale for integrating frequently and in small increments
Learn the CI pipeline flow: code push → automated build → automated test
Verify that builds and tests run automatically using a simple CI configuration such as GitHub Actions

lectures.title: CD Fundamentals — Understanding the Automated Deployment Pipeline
objectives: Understand the difference between Continuous Delivery and Continuous Deployment
Learn the pipeline flow from CI through automatic deployment to staging and production
Walk through a basic deployment pipeline: artifact → deploy → verify

lectures.title: Introduction to IaC — Managing Infrastructure as Code
objectives: Understand the problems with manual infrastructure management — configuration drift and lack of reproducibility — and the benefits IaC provides
Grasp the key concepts of declarative infrastructure and idempotency
Explore how infrastructure is defined in code through a simple IaC example such as Terraform

lectures.title: Introduction to Monitoring and Observability — Gaining Visibility into Operations
objectives: Understand the difference between monitoring and observability, and why both matter
Explain the three pillars of observability: metrics, logs, and traces
Understand how measuring and visualizing operational indicators connects to the 'Measurement' principle in DevOps

lectures.title: Containers and DevOps Tooling Overview — The Big Picture of the DevOps Ecosystem
objectives: Understand at a conceptual level why containers (Docker) play a central role in DevOps
Draw the big picture of how orchestration, CI/CD, IaC, and monitoring tools each fit into the pipeline
Identify which tools and topics to explore next after completing this introductory course
